evals.prompt: Make me a skill that helps with our database deploys. We have a runbook somewhere in this repo, and agents sometimes run the steps in the wrong order.
evals.expected_output: Before creating files, the agent searches for the runbook, drafts an intent brief covering targets, invocation, inputs, output, safety, and success criteria, then asks no more than three unresolved high-impact questions.

evals.prompt: Improve my existing review skill and fold its confirmed LEARNINGS.md corrections into the instructions. Keep how I invoke it unchanged.
evals.expected_output: The agent snapshots the old skill, preserves its name and invocation frontmatter, folds confirmed lessons in place, prunes stale prose, validates it, runs the six-message dual-window trigger check (first sentence, then first 250 characters), and compares realistic task outputs with the old version.

evals.prompt: Create a strictly Agent Skills-compliant skill that also stays explicit-only in Codex. Its Python validator depends on PyYAML.
evals.expected_output: The agent explains that strict core frontmatter cannot contain vendor invocation fields, scaffolds with --strict-core and --codex, places the Codex policy in agents/openai.yaml, uses a PEP 723 uv script for this repository, and runs both core and Codex validation profiles.
